items.title: Man's death in Bali leaves trail of debt, scores of customers in limbo

children.content: Cameron Hughes, 39, died in a Balinese immigration detention centre last week under yet-to-be-confirmed circumstances. The ABC can reveal the Perth businessman left behind hundreds of thousands of dollars in unfinished business, with dozens of customers potentially out of pocket.

gallery.altText: Cameron speaking while standing in front of a car in his workshop.
Cameron Hughes poses with a thumbs up in front of a car in his workshop.
Cameron standing in the backyard of a home with a combative expression.

thumbnail.altText: Cameron speaking while standing in front of a car in his workshop.
Cameron Hughes poses with a thumbs up in front of a car in his workshop.
Cameron standing in the backyard of a home with a combative expression.

items.title: Scale of Australia's shortage of child psychiatrists revealed in new report

children.content: Experts are warning younger children are presenting with increasingly complex mental health needs as a new report predicts a nationwide shortfall of child and adolescent psychiatrists.

image.altText: A young woman with dark shoulder-length hair look straight to camera.

items.title: Lollipop man, 87, assaulted over 'unwanted attention' to teen's girlfriend, court hears

children.content: An 18-year-old assaulted an 87-year-old traffic warden after he subjected the young man's girlfriend to "persistent unwanted attention", a Perth court hears.

image.altText: Grainy mobile phone footage of a man laying on the ground while another person stands next to him

items.title: Feral deer almost eradicated around two towns despite 'gunship' cull controversy

children.content: After a second annual aerial cull sparked controversy in southern WA, hunters say targeting feral deer from the ground is a more humane option.

gallery.altText: A red deer stares straight at the camera

thumbnail.altText: A red deer stares straight at the camera

items.title: WA's next generation of mining engineers living in tents, cars to finish studies

children.content: A damning report has found accommodation shortages at Kalgoorlie's century-old WA School of Mines are being "felt most acutely" by international and postgraduate students.

image.altText: A unviersity student outside student accommodation in Kalgoorlie.

items.title: Council to reverse decision over beachside memorial plaques for brothers

children.content: Beachside memorial plaques honouring two brothers who died in separate freak accidents will be reinstalled following their family’s battle with a local council south of Perth.

image.altText: A composite image of Wade and Ryan McCurdy.

items.title: Quick hits: Spain's secret to success, Yamal's streak and Balogun's confession

children.content: Spain showcases its biggest advantage over France, Lamine Yamal's hot streak goes on and Folarin Balogun opens up on his overturned red card. Here are today's quick hits.

articles.title: France falters at first real hurdle as superstars fail to shine

children.content: France's 2026 World Cup campaign is ended by a poor 2-0 defeat to Spain, as the much-hyped collection of star forwards fail to turn up for the biggest game yet.

image.altText: Desire Doue pulls his jersey over his face in disappointment

articles.title: Scottish traffic cone welcomed in Boston after World Cup friendship

children.content: The World Cup's obsession with Scotland fans and traffic cones continues as a witch's hat from Glasgow receives an official welcome from dignitaries in Boston.

image.altText: Massachusetts Governor Maura Healey poses wearing a traffic cone on her head next to two Scottish football fans.

articles.title: Spain soars into World Cup final with stunning upset over favourite France

children.content: Spain is through to the World Cup final thanks to a 2-0 win over France.

items.title: Tourists turn away from NT as Kakadu travellers greeted by closure signs

children.content: It is known as the jewel in the Northern Territory's tourism crown, but visitors to Kakadu National Park are being greeted by closure signs blocking roads to key waterfall sites.

image.altText: View of Twin Falls at Kakadu National Park.

items.title: Telstra slammed for putting 'burden of proof on consumers' for compensation

children.content: Telstra is assessing outage compensation claims, but consumer advocates say the process is cumbersome and puts the onus on customers rather than the telco at fault.

image.altText: Brady stands at a lectern, looking down

items.title: Shark threat closes popular beaches, snorkelling spots along Ningaloo coast

children.content: The decomposing carcass of a humpback whale closes two popular West Australian beaches as sharks converge on the area — but experts say the cause of the whale's death is of greater concern.

gallery.altText: Sharks swarm the pale carcass of a humpback whale.

thumbnail.altText: Sharks swarm the pale carcass of a humpback whale.

items.title: Buyer drops nearly $72m on one of world's most complete T-rex skeletons

children.content: One of the largest
Tyrannosaurus rex
skeletons discovered makes history as the most valuable dinosaur fossil bought at auction.

image.altText: A close-up of a tyrannosaurus rex skeleton mounted and displayed in the middle of an arid hilly landscape.

items.title: Child exploitation 'hiding in plain sight' in Thailand's sex industry

children.content: Thailand is struggling against a "hidden" world of child sexual exploitation that requires action from both the tourism industry and law enforcement agencies, according to NGOs.

image.altText: The black silhouette of a girl, with blurred lights in the background.

items.title: Jewish student tells of her disgust at TikToks in Auschwitz car park

children.content: Paris Enten has told the antisemitism royal commission her experiences of Monash University, including during an overseas trip, caused her mental health to deteriorate.

image.altText: A woman with long brown hair in a checked blazer and dark top stares off camera.

items.title: Quiz: Dust off your passport and join us on a trip

children.content: Which of these peaks isn't in the Himalayas? And in what country would you find the island of Rapa Nui?

image.altText: Four Easter Island Moai silhouettes in a single file line on a platform in front of an orange setting sun and cloudy sky

items.title: How a young doctor ended up being charged with manslaughter after street scuffle

children.content: A young Perth doctor and musician is behind bars, accused of killing a 63-year-old man after a late-night Fremantle scuffle. What do we know?

gallery.altText: Mr Joseph wearing a graduating gown and holding frame

thumbnail.altText: Mr Joseph wearing a graduating gown and holding frame

items.title: Crows defender Josh Worrell to face court over crash

children.content: Adelaide Crows defender Josh Worrell has been reported by police and will face court over a traffic crash last week.

image.altText: crows players Josh worrell, text walker and others walking off field

items.title: Smart glasses help vision-impaired despite privacy concerns

children.content: For people who are blind, AI-powered smart glasses deliver a level of independence that was once out of reach, but the rise of wearable cameras also raises urgent questions about surveillance, consent and the law.

image.altText: A close up photo of Sam's face wearing his black AI glasses.

items.title: 'They give us more power': Pogačar wins Tour stage, dismisses booing fans

children.content: Tadej Pogačar takes another step towards a fifth Tour de France win with a breakaway stage victory, with some fans booing him because of his dominance.

image.altText: The race leader in the Tour de France, wearing yellow, smiles and sits up over the line as he wins a stage.

items.title: Batteries now cheaper than gas for peak demand power supply

children.content: Falling battery costs and a data centre-fuelled dash for gas have overturned decades of accepted wisdom: gas is no longer the cheapest way to meet peak power demand.

image.altText: Flames belch form two tall metal industrial chimneys.

items.title: 'Ovulating, come over now': The seedy side of online sperm donation

children.content: Australia is facing a sperm donation shortage and it is leading those desperate to find a way to conceive down some expensive and potentially dangerous paths.
